title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Japp and Hercule Poirot interview Mr. Raleigh, a dentist whose office is located on the ground floor of the building. Raleigh states that his partner, Henry Moody, was unlikely to have committed suicide because Moody was a careful man with no debts, whereas Raleigh himself suffers from financial problems. Raleigh describes his morning patients, including Betty Hughes, Colonel Abberline, and a meticulous retired civil servant named Barnes. He also notes that an American patient named Howard Leeks, who called from the Holborn Palace Hotel, failed to show up for a special appointment. Regarding the secretary, Miss Neville, Raleigh claims she and Moody were "clean as a whistle," though he mentions that Moody had advised Neville to break off her engagement to Frank Carter. Raleigh expresses sadness over Moody's death but maintains he has no leads regarding murder.

Following the interview, Japp discusses the timeline of Moody's death, which occurred between 12:25 and 1:00 PM. Japp notes that Ambrosio, a new patient, was the last person to see Moody alive. Japp and Poirot plan to investigate several leads, including the American patient Mr. Leeks, Miss Neville’s telegram, and her relatives. They decide to visit Alistair Brent in Chelsea.

The investigation leads them to Alistair Brent's luxurious Gothic-style mansion in Chelsea. Background information reveals that Brent is a reserved man who married the wealthy Rebecca Arnold. Rebecca was the sole heir to the Rothstein and Arnold fortunes following a series of family tragedies, and despite social skepticism, her marriage to the much younger Alec Brent was successful and faithful. Japp and Poirot arrive at the mansion, where Alec Brent greets them.
